Every soda made in house, brewed on site — nothing bottled elsewhere. Stand and sip, or take it to go.

made in house brewed on site dealer's choice made in house brewed on site dealer's choice
The whole idea

DEALER'S CHOICE

You don't pick your soda. The lab picks for you. Two lines on the menu — that's the whole decision.

One beaker
ONE POUR

A full drink, about 12 oz, in a single beaker. Dealer's choice.

Three beakers on a rack
THREE POURS

Three full drinks, 8–10 oz each — variety and volume, made to share between two or three.

Want a specific soda? Just ask — same two prices. The pouring-today board shows exactly what's flowing, so you're never sipping blind.

The flavour world

Four wings. Always rotating.

Healthy sodas only, no kombucha. Everything below is just an example — the lineup never sits still.

01

Classic, made craft

A few examples: blueberry · raspberry · raspberry-vanilla · lime · cream soda.

02

Nostalgia, reworked

A few examples: orange creamsicle · cola from scratch.

03

Global roots

A few examples: goli soda · masala soda · nimbu pani · jaljeera · aam panna · rooh afza · calamansi · aguas frescas · sorrel · ginger beer · tamarind.

04

The wild wing

A few examples: chocolate soda · lychee-rose · cardamom-fig.

The space

A lab with a bar's hardware

POURING TODAY Split-flap · live
CREAM SODA
MASALA SODA
LYCHEE ROSE
NIMBU PANI

One long counter

Genius-bar style. Everything happens in front of you.

Tanks on tap

Five to ten tanks in a row, each on its own tap. Visible making.

Stand & sip

No seating. Sip at the counter or take it to go.

Second bench · ticketed

The Lab Workshop

Small groups of 8–12 taste experimental batches, build their own soda at the bench, and walk out with a corked vial of their own syrup.

Home Menu Story Workshop Visit Book a workshop
Where this came from

Our Story

A soda counter halfway across the world, a hot afternoon in 2017, and one fizzing glass that wouldn't let go.

ahmedabad, 2017 pouring since 1869 now, markham ahmedabad, 2017 pouring since 1869 now, markham
The spark

A glass at Gandhi Soda Shop

In 2017 we were in Ahmedabad, wilting in the heat, when we ducked into Gandhi Soda Shop — a counter that's been fizzing drinks by hand since 1869. Marble-stopper bottles, a rack of coloured syrups, soda pulled to order right in front of you.

One glass — sharp, cold, alive — and the idea stuck: soda as something made, not shipped. A small counter, drinks built in the open, a little theatre in every pour. We carried that home to Markham and turned it into a lab.

And this is where you come in

Your Story

You walk in curious

You didn't come with an order in mind. You see the tanks, the tap handles, the split-flap board clicking over — and you just say "surprise me." That's the whole point of dealer's choice: you get to be delighted instead of deciding.

You share the rack

Three pours land on the counter and suddenly it's a little event — you and a friend or two, trading sips, arguing over the masala soda versus the lychee-rose. Nobody's on their phone. It's soda, but it's also five good minutes.

You come back for the next drop

The board's different next weekend. There's a flavour you loved that might not return, and one you've never heard of that just did. So you keep coming — not for a usual, but for whatever's fizzing today.

made in house. dil se.

Come write the next chapter

Two lines, dealer's choice, whatever's pouring today. See you at the counter.

Home Menu Story Workshop Visit Book a workshop
Ticketed · groups of 8–12

The Lab Workshop

Get behind the counter. Taste the experiments that aren't on tap yet, build a soda that's yours, and cork a vial of your own syrup to take home.

taste the experiments build your own soda cork your syrup taste the experiments build your own soda cork your syrup
How the bench runs

An hour on the other side of the counter

01

Taste the lab

Start with a flight of experimental batches — the wild ideas still being dialed in, before they ever hit a tap.

02

Build your soda

Blend syrups, acids and botanicals at your own bench. Carbonate it. Adjust until it tastes like the one you'd order again.

03

Cork your vial

Bottle your recipe into a corked vial of syrup, labelled and yours. Make it again at home with a splash of soda water.

At a glance

Good to know

8–12
Per session

Small groups, one long counter. Great for friends, teams and birthdays.

1 vial
You take home

A corked vial of the syrup you made, labelled with your recipe.

$—
Per ticket

Pricing & dates coming soon. Tell us you're interested and we'll hold you a bench.

Round up your people

Booking opens soon. Until then, reach out and we'll put you on the list for the first sessions.
